input.headline: Parasocial' is the Cambridge dictionary word of the year but what does it mean - and are you in a parasocial relationship?

raw.headline: Parasocial' is the Cambridge dictionary word of the year but what does it mean - and are you in a parasocial relationship?

winner: I tried to end my parasocial relationship with my favorite YouTuber, but he kept sending me DMs asking if I wanted to "hang out" — I think he's still waiting for me to reply with a "hello" from the other side of the screen.

candidates.text: I asked the dictionary if I was in a parasocial relationship with my favorite singer, and it told me to stop calling him my "best friend" and start paying the rent for the apartment I've been living in inside his head.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 9.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, specific, and uses natural English with a clever twist on the concept of parasocial relationships."}

candidates.text: I asked my pet hamster, "Do we have a parasocial relationship?" He said, "I'm not your friend, I just eat your leftovers when you're not looking.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny and specific, with a clever twist on the concept of parasocial relationships using a hamster for comedic effect."}

candidates.text: I told my therapist I was in a parasocial relationship — she asked if I had a partner, and I said, "No, just a bunch of Instagram influencers who think they know me.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses a relatable scenario with a surprising twist that ties into the concept of parasocial relationships."}

candidates.text: I told my houseplant I was gonna visit next week, and now it's growing toward the window like we're in a parasocial relationship. It even wilted when I said "bye" for three whole days.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative analogy with a relatable scenario, good use of the term 'parasocial relationship,' and a touch of absurdity with the houseplant's reaction."}

candidates.text: I told my therapist I'm in a parasocial relationship with my favorite YouTuber — she said I need to either commit or get a life.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny and specific, with a clever twist on the concept of parasocial relationships; natural language and good surprise factor."}

candidates.text: I tried to end my parasocial relationship with my favorite YouTuber, but he kept sending me DMs asking if I wanted to "hang out" — I think he's still waiting for me to reply with a "hello" from the other side of the screen.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific take on parasocial relationships, with a relatable scenario and clever twist."}

candidates.text: I told my therapist I was suffering from a severe parasocial relationship with a celebrity, and she looked at me with such intense, unwavering devotion that I realized I was the one who needed to be put in a fan club.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a clever play on the therapist's reaction, concise and specific with natural English."}

candidates.text: I’m not saying my relationship with a celebrity is parasocial, but I did just get my heart broken by a man who doesn't know I exist and lives in a different zip code.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on parasocial relationships with a specific, relatable scenario and clever wordplay."}

candidates.text: I’m in a parasocial relationship with my toothpaste—it’s always there for me when I brush, but I can’t actually talk to it unless I squirt it into my mouth and hope for the best.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny and specific with a clever twist on parasocial relationships, using toothpaste as an unexpected example."}
